scope.summary: Server IAM token profiles now configure a refreshable driver TokenSource instead of embedding a one-shot JWT, and an offline TCPS counter test proves token_exec is invoked once per physical connect attempt.
out_of_scope: The D6 local TCPS lane work currently dirty in crates/oraclemcp-core/tests/oci_tcps_e2e.rs.
The live R-fail token-expiry lane with a real database and elapsed token lifetime.
Any sibling rust-oracledb driver repository change.

entry_points.name: server profile IAM token source configuration
entry_points.trace: `env -u CARGO_TARGET_DIR cargo test -p oraclemcp-core iam_token -- --nocapture` exited 0 after the B16a source changes with 45 passed; it proves config load installs a refreshable source without storing a JWT, rejects static-token/source conflicts, refuses non-TCPS before token I/O, preserves PoP key wiring, and keeps token_exec sandboxing fail-closed.

entry_points.name: physical TCPS connect token_exec counter proof
entry_points.trace: `env -u CARGO_TARGET_DIR cargo test -p oraclemcp-core --test iam_token_source_physical -- --nocapture` exited 0 at source a36dcf59 with 1 passed; it drives two real RustOracleConnection::connect attempts into a local rustls TCPS terminator and proves the counter-backed token_exec source runs exactly once per physical connect attempt, while config load runs it zero times and connect errors do not leak token or wallet material.

entry_points.name: driver TokenSource adapter bridge
entry_points.trace: `env -u CARGO_TARGET_DIR cargo test -p oraclemcp-db driver_bridge_forwards_every_refresh_request_without_caching -- --nocapture` exited 0 with 1 passed; it proves repeated driver TokenSource requests forward to the server source without caching or replacing it with a static access token.

entry_points.name: driver TokenSource failure redaction
entry_points.trace: `env -u CARGO_TARGET_DIR cargo test -p oraclemcp-db refreshable_iam_token_failure_is_typed_and_never_falls_back -- --nocapture` exited 0 with 1 passed; it proves refresh failures stay typed/redacted, do not fall back to password or static token auth, and do not render token or private-key sentinels.

limitations: This close is scoped/offline proof; it does not claim a live Oracle database token-expiry run.
The live token-expiry and rotation fault-injection lane remains tracked by oraclemcp-091-rfail-injection-lanes-ok400.
This close does not run a workspace-wide Cargo gate.
Dirty files outside this bead's source scope remain in the shared checkout; they are excluded from tree_clean by the close auditor.
